stories.headline: Willis takes the reins in Miami, and Achane's about to haul the wagon
stories.read: Well pardner, the Dolphins handed Malik Willis the saddle and his three-year paper makes him the man under center, with Tua ridin' out of town. Six career starts and a couple traded-off receivers means Miami leans on the ground game hard, so De'Von Achane just got a fatter cut of the haul, the kind of volume that pays the rent. Willis himself is cheap gold too, a late-round dart whose legs print fantasy points the way a runnin' quarterback always has. Go stake Achane high and pencil Willis as your backup streamer before the saloon catches on.

stories.headline: Olave's blood runs clean June 29, and that vein's about to gush again
stories.read: Word off the wire is Saints man Chris Olave steps off his blood thinners June 29 and walks into camp full-go, that scary clot from last winter finally behind him. Everything's gone exactly to plan, so the fella some skittish managers wrote off as a dry well is back to bein' an elite route-runner with paydirt upside. That's the play, pardner: let your leaguemates flinch at the medical talk while you grab a top-shelf receiver at a banged-up price. Draft him aggressive in every format before the herd remembers how good he is.

stories.headline: Harbaugh hauls Likely to the Giants, and Theo Johnson slides down the pecking order
stories.read: John Harbaugh brought his old Baltimore hand Isaiah Likely up to New York, and just like that Likely's the top tight end at the ranch, used inside and out. That puts Theo Johnson a notch down the pecking order, so temper that breakout talk you heard all spring, pardner. Now, the Giants may run two-tight-end sets that keep both fellas fed, but Likely's the cheap gold here while Johnson's price ought to cool. Pan that creek careful, draft Likely as a value streamer and let somebody else overpay for Johnson.
